noteDisclaimer: Tourist entry rules change often. Confirm passport validity, onward ticket, TDAC/e-gate eligibility, land vs air arrival differences, and any proof-of-funds with an official source before travel.

th.note: Residents / nationals already in Thailand — no inbound visa applies for domestic hops; carry ID/passport suitable for onward checks.

vn.note: Vietnamese passport holders commonly receive ASEAN visa‑exempt entry for Thailand (often 30 days by air — verify land-border limits separately).

my.note: Malaysian passport holders commonly receive ASEAN visa‑exempt entry (often 30 days — confirm border crossing/airport stamp rules).

sg.note: Singapore passport holders commonly receive ASEAN visa‑exempt entry (often 30 days — verify any extra exit/entry stamping requirements).

uk.note: Many UK passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days (verify current MFA/immigration bulletin; extension rules vary).

de.note: Many German passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days; confirm biometric passport and proof‑of‑funds guidelines.

fr.note: Many French passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days; confirm airport vs land rules if routed via neighbors.

nl.note: Many Dutch passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days; confirm Schengen/third‑country itineraries if layered.

se.note: Many Swedish passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days.

us.note: Many US passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days; confirm any prior overstay/incident flags ahead of ticketing.

ca.note: Many Canadian passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days.

au.note: Many Australian passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days.

nz.note: Many New Zealand passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days.

jp.note: Many Japanese passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days.

kr.note: Many South Korean passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays up to ~60 days.

hk.note: HKSAR (and BN(O) where applicable per current rules) often receives visa‑exempt entry for tourism — verify exact passport type and stamping practice.

cn.note: Temporary visa‑exemption programs for Chinese passports have rotated; assume you must verify the currently published duration and prerequisites before ticketing.

in.note: India has repeatedly been granted temporary Thai visa exemptions (duration has moved between 30/60 days): confirm embassy/MFA bulletin for exact dates and onward ticket docs.

ae.note: UAE passports commonly qualify for extended Thai visa‑exemption tourist stays; verify any layover visa needs if returning via tighter GCC transit rules.

br.note: Many Brazilian passports qualify for Thai visa‑exemption tourism programs (historically up to ~60 days in recent schemes — confirm MFA list inclusion).

za.note: South African passports are frequently included on Thai visa‑exemption traveler lists — confirm stamping duration if you arrive from high‑risk-route hubs.
